prompt:
An 80 mm long 3D printed PLA fincan for a 35 mm model rocket stands on a table, with four symmetrical fins radiating from the lower section of the cylinder. The fins are thin yet sturdy, with crisp edges, and the interior bore is sized to slide over a 35 mm body tube while the base opening clearly accepts an 18 mm motor. The lightweight construction is evident from the thin walls and sparse internal ribbing visible at the base, suggesting a mass around 12 g.

The scene is a near-future solarpunk rocketry bench with soft, even lighting, nearby body tubes and nose cones in the blurred background, and a compact 3D printer off to one side. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or readable markings, and a composition that centers the fincan’s fins, length, and motor opening while the rest of the workspace remains softly out of focus, emphasizing its role as a simple, modular rocket base.
